title: Model, Surface, Capability, and Constraint Selection · Failure Triage
summary: Choose between Claude and Codex surfaces for a repository and document task. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a capability and surface decision matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.
Verify current model and tool capabilities from official documentation rather than remembered names.
Match reasoning effort and autonomy to task complexity, reversibility, and verification cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Use a changed debug case for prompt model surface that tests this competency: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed debug case for prompt model surface that tests this competency: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose chat, coding-agent, API, or workflow surfaces according to state, tools, latency, and review needs. Artifact under review: a capability and surface decision matrix.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Surface Fit without its stated preconditions, omit the a capability and surface decision matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Surface Fit for a capability and surface decision matrix.
The record names the assumption, applies Surface Fit, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Surface Fit record says a capability and surface decision matrix looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-provider-portability-1sl221" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Provider Portability into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-provider-portability-1sl221
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Provider Portability structural check is only one gate. a capability and surface decision matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate stable task contracts from provider-specific syntax and capability assumptions.
